paragraphs.body: The Zhengzhou Airport Economy Zone (ZAEZ) is a national-level economic development area in Henan, China, centered on Zhengzhou Xinzheng International Airport. Established in 2013 with approval from the State Council, it is the first airport-based economic zone in China. The zone’s development is intended to integrate the airport with industrial, logistics, and urban growth, driving regional economic transformation and enhancing Henan’s connectivity to global markets[1]. It often referred as "Chinese Aerotropolis," the ZAEZ covers an area of approximately 415 km² around the airport.

Role of the Airport in Regional Development Zhengzhou Xinzheng International Airport serves as the engine of the ZAEZ’s growth and regional influence[3]. Designated one of China’s eight Category 1 airports, Xinzheng Airport has rapidly become a major air hub in central China. Its strategic location near the geographic center of China enables efficient access to major domestic markets, and it was chosen as the Asia hub for Cargolux Airlines and a focus city for carriers like China Southern Airlines[4]. As the core of the aerotropolis, the airport’s expansion has been pivotal: a second runway and a new 360,000 m² Terminal 2 opened in 2015–2016, making Zhengzhou the first airport in central China with dual runways and terminals. By 2018 the airport was ranked 12th nationwide in passenger volume and 7th in cargo and reflecting its emergence as a key national aviation gateway. This growth in air connectivity – including dozens of freight routes linking Zhengzhou to Europe, North America, and Asia – underpins the region's aviation-driven economic development. The airport has also become a central node of the country's "Air Silk Road," facilitating international trade as part of the Belt and Road Initiative[5]. The Air Silk Road’s development coincided with Zhengzhou' s designation as one of China's first cross-border e-commerce pilot zones, fueling growth in cross-border e-commerce. Efficient air logistics and supportive policies (such as a Free Trade Zone and "single-window" customs clearance) have enabled a “buy globally, sell globally" model from Zhengzhou. Notably, Luxembourg’s Cargolux, a partner in the Air Silk Road, made Zhengzhou one of its key Asian hubs.[6]

Regional Economic Development and Employment Air Silk Road has delivered broad economic benefits for Zhengzhou and the surrounding region. It strengthened Henan’s status as a national transportation hub and gateway for trade. The initiative has been leveraged to promote industrial upgrading and export-oriented growth in the local economy. Logistics and e-commerce have become pillar industries for Zhengzhou, spurring the development of warehousing, freight forwarding, and associated services. The Zhengzhou Airport Economy Zone now hosts clusters of modern logistics and cross-border e-commerce firms, as well as manufacturing bases oriented toward international markets. These activities generated thousands of jobs in aviation logistics, retail distribution, and industry. For example, the city’s automotive and equipment manufacturing clusters expanded alongside the air hub; by 2019 an automotive production cluster in the airport zone achieved an output of over US$1.5 billion. In Luoyang (an adjacent city integrated into Henan’s airport economy plan), new industrial clusters supported by improved transport links now contribute over half of that city’s industrial output and employment.[6] By linking a once landlocked province to global supply chains, the Air Silk Road has increased foreign trade and attracted capital to central China. Local authorities credit it with accelerating Henan's "open economy" development and raising the region's international profile. In summary, the Zhengzhou–Luxembourg Air Silk Road has helped transform Zhengzhou into an inland international logistics hub, boosted cross-border commerce, and fostered new industries and jobs in the region. Its success is often cited as a model of how improved air connectivity under the Belt and Road Initiative can drive regional economic growth and integration into global markets.

References 1) "China's Aerotropolis: The Zhengzhou Airport Economy Zone". International Airport Review. Retrieved 2025-02-27. 2) "ZHENGZHOU TAKES OFF". ACI Asia-Pacific Airports. 2016-09-07. Retrieved 2025-02-27. 3) "Zhengzhou Airport Economy Zone forms "1+1+7" port network for B&R participation - Xinhua Silk Road". en.imsilkroad.com. "Analysis of the Evolution of Foreign Trade Patterns and Influencing Factors in Henan Province from 2002 to 2021". Sustainability. 15 (21): 15341. doi:10.3390/su152115341. ISSN 2071-1050 5)
